rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PEARL A. HENNIGH TRUST DTD JUNE 22, 1999, AS AMENDED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Professional fiduciary Daniel Cunningham is requesting $29,526.35 in fees as successor trustee of the trust. Daniel J. Cunningham paid himself most of the fees on account. Daniel Cunningham is also requesting $12,528.00 in fees as case manager. Daniel Cunningham already paid himself most of the case manager fees without court authority. Daniel Cunningham requests the remaining $678.85 be paid. Daniel Cunningham resigned as case manager on 7/23/2018 but is requesting $913.50 in fees after date of resignation. 2) Page 7 of the petition lines 13-16 alleges professional fiduciary Daniel Cunningham paid himself $35,423.50 in error and Daniel Cunningham will repay the Trust $34,744.65 prior to hearing. The 01/12/2021 Supplement attached a copy of a cashier's check and receipt alleging $35,423.50 was deposited back to the estate. 3) The 05/07/2019 Order Settling Second Amended First Account granted attorney fees through 8/31/2019. Attorney is requesting fees in this account fees between 8/27/2018 and 8/31/2018 totaling $832.50. 4) Petitioner is requesting re-authorization for funds held in Diversified Income Strategy: Vanguard Wellesley Income Index Fund, Vanguard High Dividend Yield Index Fund Admiral Share Class and Vanguard International High Dividend Yield Index Fund. 5) On Disbursements Schedule C (page 4 of 12) the schedules alleges Christopher Valenti was paid $2,062.50. The invoice alleges $1,150.50 earned. On page 2 of the supplement Petitioner alleges there was an overpayment to Christopher Valenti in the amount of $915.00. The Petitioner has contacted Mr. Valenti to inquire.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANZ VON UCKERMANN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/27/21 to follow adjudication of the FIRST accounting petition, which was continued AGAIN to 3/23/21 for lack of courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] It is also noted that all four Corrected I&As filed on 10/6/20 were signed by just one of the two co-executors, Robert Noelke, and he also failed to properly verify each of them (undated). (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed.) It is also noted that (1) the Proof of Service filed on 1/13/21 was incomplete at item 3, (2) amounts far exceeding the FDIC limit are being held at one bank, and (3) no statement by the attorney or fiduciary appears to have been filed yet regarding who will retain the original financial statements and for the necessary amount of time, as required by LRSF 2.11 M.4 and 14.60 D. Any costs for which an attorney or fiduciary seeks reimbursement must clearly indicate the date, payee, description, and amount. Filing fees or costs without any indication of what was filed or CourtCall appearance when there was no hearing may not be allowed. Courtesy copies and a proposed order, where applicable, must always be provided at least FIVE Court days before a hearing. [LRSF 14.7.] This has been noted repeatedly in tentative rulings by Examiners. An amended petition "completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes" [CRC 7.3(3).] and should neither refer to issues raised by an Examiner or attach an "answer sheet" as an exhibit to it. All pleadings should stand on their own and not refer to Examiner's Notes or tentative rulings.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner alleges that the principal place of administration is Escondido, CA. 2) There is a 3-year gap between the former trustee's last accounting and the instant accounting. 3) Trustee requests 1% guideline fees of $15,875.45. He alleges that he has spent over 75 hours on this matter, which includes 14 hours for a court appearance. 4) Objections have been filed. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 9th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ULES JACKSON AND MATTIE W. JACKSON REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Transfer Of Assets To Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) There has been no filed response to the following examiner note for the 850 petition: a. The petition does not explain who the heirs at law are or if they received notice. [Prob. C. 851(b)]. 2) At the 03/09/2020 hearing the Court ordered Mr. Goins to file a joint status report with courtesy copies delivered 1 week before the next hearing date. The Court further noted that in the future, sanctions will be imposed, that parties need to do their parts. Substitution of Attorney filed on 03/12/2020 by Endy Ukoha-Ajiike replacing Vernon Goins as new attorney for Barbara Hill. A petition for approval of joint status report was filed 11/30/2020. 3) Objections have been filed. Objections are made to the accounting only being for two years. The 07/18/2018 court order ordered Carolyn Hill to account from 04/12/12 to present. This order appears to order a 6-year account if just provided to the beneficiaries as the court requires accountings to conform to LRSF 14.35. 4) The court does not have a proposed order for the First Account and Report of Successor Trustee.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Betty Williams Petition For Partition
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/11/2020 to be heard after law and motion. Issues: 1) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. 2) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD DIAMOND
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/6/2021. Issues: 1) Petitioner must file an ex parte petition to increase bond and follow LRSF 14.11 B. Immediately upon the occurrence of facts making it necessary or appropriate to increase the amount of the bond, the personal representative must make an ex parte application for an order increasing the bond. If the personal representative has not already made application under (a), the attorney for the personal representative must make the ex parte application immediately upon becoming aware of the need to increase bond. [CRC 7.204, LRSF 14.11, 14.16]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JUSTIN WINFREY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Letters Of Administration
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MIKHAIL M. VAYNBERG AND BEATRISA VAYNBERG LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Interest In Trust Property, Directing Trustees To Transfer Possession Of Property, Accounting, Damages, And Attorney'S
rulings.Rulings: continued to 4/20/21. an amended petition was filed 3/3/21. an amended petition requires the same notice as the petition it amends (30 days). please note, the POS filed 3/1/21 is not valid as the date of the hearing was shown as 3/9/21, and it was filed before the pleading requiring notice was filed. no later than noon on 4/10/21, a file stamped copy of the petition and a proposed order must be delivered to the examiner in room 202.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DONALD L. YOST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining That Certain Assets Are Included In Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/13/21. Still no courtesy copy of the petition. A copy of the trust does not appear to have been attached to the petition. The proof of service was for the wrong form of Notice (as noted in the tentative ruling for the 2/9/21 hearing). If courtesy copyies of the petition, supplement to the petition (submitting a copy of the trust), and proof of service of the correct Notice are not received by the assigned Examiner by 4/2/21, an appearance will be required on 4/13/21 for the Court to determine if the petition should be taken off calendar. (Notice is also required for a supplement to a trust in connection with an 850 petition.)
